entries.evidence_summary: Authority is a strict precedence chain with operative layers first and reference evidence separated explicitly.
Material pushback is defined once in <guardrails> and reused consistently for freeze and terminal-state logic.
Canonical artifacts are separated from run-scoped evidence with distinct lifecycle rules and promotion behavior.
Each terminal state has an explicit, testable trigger tied to run evidence and artifact writes.
Step 3 requires executed-check evidence, semantic scope coverage, and forbids claiming checks that did not actually run.
Out-of-scope exclusions are deduplicated, normalized, and listed once per concept.
Arbitration has a mechanical trigger, actor, required evidence, and constrained outcome limited to minor/suggestion disagreements.
All required canonical package files appear with full tool-relative paths; no basename-only references remain.
Completion is defined separately for frozen, frozen_with_conditions, pushback, blocked, and resume_required.
Context gathering is labeled as preconditions before Step 1 rather than part of the numbered execution flow.
Carried-forward findings must be tracked by conceptual-group ID in the fix-items map and in subsequent rounds.
Decision-log preservation is defined as append-only on freeze, and board-summary preservation is defined via run-scoped copies plus canonical replacement on freeze only.
Each numbered step states whether it describes current inherited workflow or target-state contract behavior.
Universal round and terminal artifacts are always written in applicable states and are not gated behind revision-only conditions.
The outputs section is an authoritative artifact matrix with artifact class, exact path, and lifecycle per operation and terminal state.

Local authority order and review behavior are derived from the COO, runtime contract, component review contract, shared review contract, and rulebook, with shared policy referenced rather than reinvented.
The mission statement describes the enduring purpose of the role package rather than the current run state.
The write boundary is explicit and rooted in the active runtime contract: canonical outputs under tools/agent-role-builder/role/ and run evidence under tools/agent-role-builder/runs/<job-id>/.
Only operative governance sources appear in the authority chain, while architecture docs are classified separately as reference evidence.
Governance mechanics are included only where inherited from declared contracts or authority docs, and the role definition limits itself to component-local consequences.
Use-when items are positive inclusions and not-in-scope items are pure exclusions with no mixed polarity bullets.
The governed review round is an ordered sub-sequence tied to per-round artifacts including compliance maps, fix-items maps, review results, learning output, diff summary, and post-round evidence.
Component-local governance files are listed explicitly, and inherited runtime obligations are referenced from the shared and component review contracts.
Required and conditionally required inputs enumerate the external prerequisites needed for authority validation, review execution, and terminal decisions.
